body_txt: A comedy trope where, quite simply, a character is given an opportunity by a genie, witch, or magical artifact to Make a Wish or three , and wastes them on something stupid and/or frivolous. Sandwiches seem to be a particular favorite. If it's an Accidental Wish , this may be because the character was just talking about what he "wishes," without really knowing that he was in the presence of some magical power that would grant his wish despite how wasteful it is (or perhaps, his ignorance consisted in a mistake such as thinking the power was unlimited instead of just Three Wishes ); or he may have some Skewed Priorities that make him ask for something that makes little sense as a use of the unlimited power of the Wish. A lot of times, this will be used to circumvent Jackass Genies or Literal Genies , since, with a smaller reward from a wish, possible negative consequences from the reward will also generally be smaller. If this works and the person who wished for something small ends up happier than the one who wished for ten bajillion dollars and a mansion full of Playboy models, then expect An Aesop on greed and being satisfied with simpler pleasures in life. In cases where the wish-granter is disposable after its wishes are used up , if The Hero doesn't have more pressing things to wish for, they may invoke this trope simply to ensure there's nothing left for the Big Bad to use - dangerous artifact disposal and a knee-slapping reaction from the villain in one motion. Even wasted, these wishes can be subject to Be Careful What You Wish For . Expect to find someone Lamenting Wasted Potential when they hear someone was wasteful with their wish. Also see Mundane Wish , when the wish is intentionally mundane because the wisher doesn't need or want anything grandiose. Contrast Ludicrous Gift Request .
